sections.paragraph:
παραυτά (also πάραυτα) adv. (for πάρʼ αὐτά sc. πράγματα; Aeschyl., Demosth. et al.; Vett. Val. 152, 10; PTebt 13, 15 [II b.c.]; PLips 36, 6; New Docs 3, no. 101, 11; PGM 4, 2071 al. in pap) pert. to a point of time immediately subsequent to another point of time, on the spot, at once ITr 11:1.—KKontos in Ἀθηνᾶ 6, 1894, 369.—M-M s.v. παραυτίκα.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 772
